intro.heading: Hi, Grex!
intro.tagline: AI generates the code. Grex is where you orchestrate, review, and ship it.

agents.heading: Log in to your agents
agents.description: Grex uses your local Claude Code and Codex login sessions. You can log in now, or continue and log in later.

repositoryCli.heading: Connect accounts
repositoryCli.description: Each repo uses one of your accounts. Add now or skip — existing logins are picked up automatically. All accounts live in your local gh/glab CLI.
repositoryCli.finishAuthToast: Finish {{provider}} CLI auth, then click Set up again.
repositoryCli.gitlabDomainHelp: Use gitlab.com or your self-hosted GitLab domain.

skills.heading: Power up Grex
skills.description: Grex is installing the CLI and skills in the background so it can split work, run agents, call tools, and carry context across your workspaces.
skills.setupFailed: Something went wrong — don't worry, Grex will work fine without it.

cli.label: Grex CLI
cli.description: Control Grex from your terminal: create workspaces, send prompts, inspect files, and script repeatable flows.

grexSkills.label: Grex Skills (Beta)
grexSkills.description: Install skills so Grex can help with more workflows across every workspace.

repoImport.heading: Bring in your first repositories
repoImport.description: Start with a local project, or pull a remote repository from GitHub. You can add more than one before entering Grex.
repoImport.chooseLocalDescription: Add a folder already on this machine.
repoImport.addingLocalDescription: Pick a folder, then we'll wire it up.
repoImport.importFromGithubDescription: Clone a remote project into Grex.
repoImport.emptyState: Choose a local folder or import from GitHub to build your first queue.

conversation.userPrompt: Split this feature into three workspaces — DB migration, backend handlers, and frontend wiring — so I can pair on each in parallel.
conversation.assistantReply: Spun up three workspaces — switch between them in the sidebar to keep each lane moving.
conversation.composerPlaceholder: Ask to make changes, @mention files, run /commands
